name: Logout from the sidebar (any authenticated page) clears the session and lands on /
description: DashSidebar's Log out button POSTs /api/auth/logout and redirects to /. Backend invalidates the session token; the pm_session cookie is cleared (or replaced with empty + Max-Age=0). After logout, a request to a protected route 302s to / (auth middleware kicks in).

persona.name: Diego
persona.context: Wants to be sure the session is closed before he leaves the office laptop.

preconditions: User authenticated, on any of the 9 authenticated pages

steps.action: Clicks Log out at the bottom of the sidebar
steps.expect: POST /api/auth/logout → backend session-store deletes the token; cookie pm_session is cleared via Set-Cookie: pm_session=; Max-Age=0; window redirects to /
Public landing page renders; no logged-in shell, no leaked greeting/name, no auth-only sidebar

steps.action: Manually types /dashboard in the URL bar
steps.expect: Auth middleware sees no session, 302s to / immediately — no flash of skeleton or shell
Returns to / (no leaked dashboard from history); refreshing /dashboard in any cached tab also redirects to /

evaluationCriteria.check: Logout transition feels intentional — brief loading state allowed, but no half-rendered authed pages
Network blip during logout doesn't leave the user in an undefined state — the FE's redirect-anyway pattern catches that
Session token is invalidated server-side, not just cookie-cleared client-side; reusing the captured cookie value in another tool fails 401/403
Logout button has type=button (not submit), proper button semantics; redirect happens via JS so screen readers announce the new page
